surface_forms: the attacks on the World Trade Center
the attack on the World Trade Center
description: 2001 attacks in the United States
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Pennsylvania, officially the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, is a U.S. state located in the Mid-Atlantic, Northeastern, Appalachian, and Great Lakes regions of the United States.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

aliases: Staten Island borough, New York City
description: borough of New York City, New York, United States of America
description_wiki: Staten Island  ( STAT-ən) is the southernmost of the five boroughs of New York City, coextensive with Richmond County and situated at the southernmost point of the State of New York.

description: highway in New Jersey
description_wiki: The Garden State Parkway (GSP) is a controlled-access toll road that stretches the north–south length of eastern New Jersey from the state's southernmost tip near Cape May north to the New York state line at Montvale.

description: city in New Jersey, United States
description_wiki: Jersey City is the second-most populous city in the U.S. state of New Jersey, after Newark.

description: city in Hudson County, New Jersey, United States
description_wiki: Bayonne ( bay-(Y)OHN) is a city in Hudson County in the U.S. state of New Jersey, in the Gateway Region on Bergen Neck, a peninsula between Newark Bay to the west, the Kill Van Kull to the south, and New York Bay to the east.

description: state highway in Hudson and Middlesex counties in New Jersey, United States
description_wiki: Route 440 is a state highway in New Jersey, United States.

description: United States military base
description_wiki: Fort Wadsworth is a former United States military installation on Staten Island in New York City, situated on The Narrows, which divide New York Bay into Upper and Lower bays, a natural point for defense of the Upper Bay, Manhattan, and beyond.

description: security and rescue military service branch for U.S. waters
description_wiki: The United States Coast Guard (USCG) is the maritime security, search and rescue, and law enforcement service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: neighborhood in the northeastern part of Staten Island, New York City, USA
description_wiki: Rosebank is a neighborhood in northeastern Staten Island, one of New York City's five boroughs.

description: island in New York Harbor in the United States of America
description_wiki: Ellis Island is an island in New York Harbor, within the U.S. states of New Jersey and New York.

description: borough of New York City, New York, United States
description_wiki: Manhattan is the most densely populated and geographically smallest of the five boroughs of New York City.

description: tunnel in New York City
description_wiki: The Hugh L. Carey Tunnel, commonly referred to as the Brooklyn–Battery Tunnel, Battery Tunnel or Battery Park Tunnel, is a tolled tunnel in New York City that connects Red Hook in Brooklyn with the Battery in Manhattan.

description: island in Manhattan, New York, United States
description_wiki: Governors Island is a 172-acre (70 ha) island in New York Harbor, within the New York City borough of Manhattan.

aliases: New York-Newark-Jersey City, NY-NJ-PA Metropolitan Statistical Area
New York-Newark-Jersey City, NY-NJ Metro Area
New York-Newark-Jersey City metropolitan statistical area
New York-Newark-Jersey City, NY-NJ Metropolitan Statistical Area
New York-Newark-Jersey City, NY-NJ-PA Metro Area
description: most populous metropolitan area in the United States
description_wiki: The New York metropolitan area, also called the Tri-State area and sometimes referred to as Greater New York or Metro New York, is the largest metropolitan economy in the world, with a gross metropolitan product of over US $2.6 trillion.

description: uniformed federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The United States Park Police (USPP) is the oldest uniformed federal law enforcement agency in the United States.

description: U.S. federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The United States Secret Service (USSS or Secret Service) is a federal law enforcement agency under the United States Department of Homeland Security.

description: architectural structure
description_wiki: Chelsea Piers is a series of piers in Chelsea, on the West Side of Manhattan in New York City.

description: federal law enforcement agency of the United States
description_wiki: The United States Marshals Service (USMS) is a federal law enforcement agency under the United States Department of Justice.

aliases: Statue of Liberty World Heritage Site
description: island in the Upper New York Bay, United States
description_wiki: Liberty Island is a federally owned island in Upper New York Bay in the northeastern United States.
